title: Emission (signal + statistic, the L1 canonical shape)
description: One envelope for everything that happens in the lab. Bus semantics (signal) and lake semantics (statistic) are the same instance: every emission fans out to scoped subscribers AND lands durably in the lake. The semantic layer (L2) is auto-derived from these.

type.description: Dot-namespaced event/statistic name (e.g. doorman.link.flap, host.cpu.pct). The semantic-layer registry key; first emission of a new type auto-registers it.

ts.description: When it happened (producer clock, RFC 3339 UTC).

service.description: Emitting service (manager, dispatcher, control-plane, box-agent, console-api, bridge, ...).

host.description: Canonical .N host label, mc34, or VPS.

agent.description: Canonical agent handle when the emission is about/by an agent.

subject.description: What the emission is about — an entity key, joinable (agent handle, host, service name, task id, card id, item id).

severity.description: First-class severity. p0 cracks the facade; interrupt tier is reserved for p0/safety/principal-command.

action.description: Optional: what a human/agent should do about it (the SignalRow action line).

task_id.description: Tracker tie whenever the activity belongs to a task.

scope.description: ReBAC visibility tag. REQUIRED — un-scoped emissions are rejected (fail-loud, never default-public).

meta.description: Optional typing hints consumed by L2 auto-derivation. Unknown keys tolerated.
